name: Folded Dipole
description: A folded dipole consists of two parallel conductors connected at their ends, with one conductor fed at the center and the other acting as a parasitic element. This configuration provides higher input impedance (typically 300 ohms) compared to a simple dipole while maintaining similar radiation characteristics. The folded dipole is widely used in applications requiring impedance matching to higher impedance transmission lines.

length.name: Total Length
length.description: Total length of each conductor in the folded dipole

spacing.name: Conductor Spacing
spacing.description: Distance between the two parallel conductors

stepUpRatio.name: Step-up Ratio
stepUpRatio.description: Impedance transformation ratio compared to simple dipole

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves determining conductor dimensions, spacing, and feed point configuration to achieve desired impedance and radiation characteristics while maintaining resonance at the operating frequency.

steps.title: Determine Operating Wavelength
steps.description: Calculate free-space wavelength at the design frequency

steps.title: Calculate Initial Length
steps.description: Set initial conductor length to half wavelength with velocity factor correction

steps.title: Determine Conductor Spacing
steps.description: Choose spacing based on desired impedance and mechanical constraints

steps.title: Calculate Wire Radius
steps.description: Select wire radius for practical construction and impedance control
steps.formula: a = lambda / (2 * pi * 100) to lambda / (2 * pi * 50)

steps.title: Compute Input Impedance
steps.description: Calculate theoretical input impedance using transmission line theory

steps.title: Adjust for End Effects
steps.description: Apply length correction for finite wire radius and end effects

equations.inputImpedance: Z_in = 4 * Z_0 * sqrt(1 + (d/(2*a))^2) where Z_0 = 73 ohms

references: Balanis, C.A., 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Wiley, 2016
Stutzman, W.L. and Thiele, G.A., 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Wiley, 2012
Kraus, J.D. and Marhefka, R.J., 'Antennas for All Applications', 3rd Edition, McGraw-Hill, 2002
Volakis, J.L., 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, McGraw-Hill, 2007
